div1.entryFree: Cuckoo, the bird Cuculus: --s hatch in sparrows' nests, Lucr. 849. LLL V, 2, 896. Merch. V, 112. H4A III, 2, 75. as that ungentle gull, the --'s bird, useth the sparrow, V, 1, 60. the hedge-sparrow fed the c. so long, that it's had it head bit off by it young, Lr. I, 4, 235. the c. builds not for himself, Ant. II, 6, 28. Used as a term of contempt: o' horseback, ye c., but afoot he will not budge a foot, H4A II, 4, 387. -- Its note prognosticating the destiny of cuckoldom: LLL V, 2, 908 etc. Mids. III, 1, 134. Mids. III, 1, 134 All's I, 3, 67.
